headline: Does GM’s EV Slowdown and Truck Pivot Reshape the Bull Case for General Motors (GM)?
summary: In recent days, General Motors’ board approved record compensation for CEO Mary Barra, authorized a one-time US$40.00 million award for chief product officer Sterling Anderson, acquired a new plant to support future gasoline-powered pickup and Escalade production, and reportedly postponed its next-generation full-size electric truck program at Factory Zero. Together, these moves point to a meaningful rebalancing of GM’s priorities between electric vehicles and high-margin trucks while...
